model.label: GLM-5
model.description: Z.AI • New-generation foundation model capable of providing reliable productivity in long-range agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Arizona enters as a legitimate national championship contender ranked 3rd in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite two-way profile—7th in adjusted offense and 3rd in adjusted defense—while Arkansas sits 17th-20th in the major metrics, representing an efficiency margin gap of roughly 12 points per 100 possessions. The decisive mismatch is Arizona's elite defense (3rd nationally) against an Arkansas team that ranks just 50th in adjusted defensive efficiency, meaning the Wildcats can both score with the Razorbacks and get stops when needed. Arizona's star freshman Koa Peat has returned from his February injury and scored 21 points in the Big 12 championship victory over Houston, while Jaden Bradley earned Big 12 Player of the Year honors anchoring a roster that has won 11 straight. Arkansas's SEC tournament championship and Darius Acuff Jr.'s 30-point title game performance show they're dangerous, but their defensive vulnerabilities against an Arizona team that can dominate on both ends makes this a manageable 1 vs 4 matchup with limited upset risk.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Arkansas Sweet 16 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Compared team ratings, injury status, and matchup dynamics to determine Arizona's substantial analytical and defensive advantages over Arkansas.

evidence.detail: Arizona ranks 3rd in both metrics with +37.56 efficiency margin; Arkansas ranks 17th-20th with +25.43 margin—a gap of ~12 points per 100 possessions
Arizona's adjusted defense ranks 3rd nationally (91.1) vs Arkansas's 50th (102.0)—a 47-spot gap that should prove decisive
Both teams have elite offenses: Arizona 7th (127.2), Arkansas 5th (127.6)—essentially even
Koa Peat returned from leg injury and scored 21 points in Big 12 championship; Darius Acuff Jr. returned from ankle injury for SEC tournament
Arizona won Big 12 tournament championship; Arkansas won SEC tournament championship with Acuff Jr. scoring 30 points in final

result.text: Tommy Lloyd provides Dwayne Aristode and Koa Peat injury updates Skip to main content About Zona News Football Basketball Wildcats Baseball Cats in the Pros FanSided NCAA Sites Follow Us Share on X About Zona News Football Wildcats Football Wildcats Football Recruiting Basketball Wildcats Basketball Wildcats Basketball Recruiting Wildcats Baseball Cats in the Pros FanSided NCAA Sites Share on X Tommy Lloyd provides Dwayne Aristode and Koa Peat injury updates Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd provided updates on freshmen forwards Dwayne Aristode and Koa Peat after the loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. By Alan Rubenstein | Share on Facebook Share on X Share on Reddit Copy Article Add us as a preferred source Arizona Wildcats forward Koa Peat (10) awaits for an inbound pass during the game against Kansas Jayhawks inside Allen Fieldhouse on Feb. 9, 2026. | Evert Nelson/The Capital-Journal / USA TODAY NETWORK via Imagn Images Arizona was without freshmen forwards Dwayne Aristode for the entire game and Koa Peat for the second half in the loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. Aristode was out with an illness and Peat sat out the second half with a leg injury, according to Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd in the post game. --> Arizona was without freshmen forwards Dwayne Aristode for the entire game and Koa Peat for the second half in the loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. Aristode was out with an illness and Peat sat out the second half with a leg injury, according to Arizona head coach Tommy Lloyd in the post game. Peat had consecutive games below 10 points for the first time this season. Playing 11 minutes, Peat scored two points without taking a shot from the field and had one rebound as the game was tied at 32 at halftime. Peat has a lower leg injury that Arizona trainer Justin Kokoskie and doctors will examine. --> Peat had consecutive games below 10 points for the first time this season. Playing 11 minutes, Peat scored two points without taking a shot from the field and had one rebound as the game was tied at 32 at halftime. Peat has a lower leg injury that Arizona trainer Justin Kokoskie and doctors will examine. Aristode has an undisclosed illness that Lloyd described as " nothing crazy," but that will keep him out for a short while. When further asked if Aristode will play against BYU on Wednesday, Lloyd replied, "I don't know for sure...but I think there's a chance." --> Aristode has an undisclosed illness that Lloyd described as " nothing crazy," but that will keep him out for a short while. When further asked if Aristode will play against BYU on Wednesday, Lloyd replied, "I don't know for sure...but I think there's a chance." Senior forward Tobe Awaka started in place of Peat in the second half. Lloyd played seven players in the first half and six in the second without Peat. Five Arizona players played at least 37 minutes in the 78-75 overtime loss to Texas Tech on Saturday. --> Senior forward Tobe Awaka star
